name: Moravian-Silesian Region (Czechia)
description: A banner of the quartered Moravian-Silesian arms — the black Silesian eagle on gold, the chequy Moravian eagle on blue, and the horse of Ostrava.
history: The self-governing regions were created on 1 January 2000; like all Czech regional flags this is a banner of the quartered regional arms, adopted in the early 2000s.

features.role: quartered banner of the regional coat of arms
the black Silesian eagle with a silver crescent on gold
the red-and-silver chequy Moravian eagle on blue
